normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem bst_insert_terminates (t : Tree) (k : Int) : ∃ n : Nat, True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive definition is well-founded on the structure of the input tree (i.e., recursion terminates).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_shape (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  match t with
  | Tree.nil => bst_insert t k = Tree.node Tree.nil k Tree.nil
  | Tree.node l x r =>
      match k.cmp x with
      | .lt => bst_insert t k = Tree.node (bst_insert l k) x r
      | .gt => bst_insert t k = Tree.node l x (bst_insert r k)
      | .eq => bst_insert t k = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Unfolding the implementation: on Nil it creates a single-node tree; on Node it recurses left/right depending on comparison, and leaves the tree unchanged on duplicates.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_noop_on_duplicate (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  (∃ l r, t = Tree.node l k r) → bst_insert t k = t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the tree already contains a node with key k at the root, insertion of k is a no-op (the implementation returns the original node).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_preserves_bst (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t → BST (bst_insert t k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If the input tree satisfies the binary search tree ordering invariant, then the output tree also satisfies it.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_ordering (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (∀ x, x ∈ keys (bst_insert t k) →
    (x < rootKey (bst_insert t k) ∨ x = rootKey (bst_insert t k) ∨ x > rootKey (bst_insert t k)))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: All keys in the resulting BST respect the BST ordering relative to the root (capturing that insertion places the new key in the correct side).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_mem_inserted (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t → k ∈ keys (bst_insert t k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: After insertion, the key k is present in the resulting tree (either newly inserted or already existed, in which case the tree is unchanged).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_mem_preserves_others (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (∀ x, x ≠ k → x ∈ keys t → x ∈ keys (bst_insert t k))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Insertion does not remove any existing key other than possibly leaving it unchanged; all keys different from k remain present.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_no_new_duplicate (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  BST t →
  (countKeys (bst_insert t k) k = countKeys t k)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Because the implementation is a no-op on duplicates, the number of occurrences of k does not increase.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem bst_insert_correct_by_cases (t : Tree) (k : Int) :
  match t with
  | Tree.nil => True
  | Tree.node l x r =>
      (k < x → bst_insert t k = Tree.node (bst_insert l k) x r) ∧
      (k > x → bst_insert t k = Tree.node l x (bst_insert r k)) ∧
      (k = x → bst_insert t k = t)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The output matches the intended recursive BST insertion behavior for all comparison cases.
